description_long: This object is a detailed representation of a reddish-orange crustacean, likely a lobster or crayfish, featuring a segmented body with a hard exoskeleton. The cephalothorax is robust and slightly arched, transitioning into a long, narrow abdomen that ends in a fan-shaped tail. Prominent claws are visible at the front, with multiple jointed walking legs extending from the underside. Two long, thin antennae project forward from the head, and small, dark eyes are positioned on short stalks. The surface appears hard and slightly glossy, with subtle textural ridges and color gradations from deep red to lighter orange and beige.
description: A reddish-orange crustacean with a segmented body, large claws, long antennae, and a hard, glossy exoskeleton.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the crustacean, highlighting the segmented body, long tail, and extended claws.
description_view_1: Front view emphasizing the symmetry of the claws, the forward-facing antennae, and the width of the cephalothorax.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, displaying the other profile of the body and legs.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tail fan and the alignment of the abdomen segments.
